topics.title: The Strange Case of Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde

plot.reason: This immutable additive shard targets other OCR J352 options.

quotation.reason: This immutable additive shard targets other OCR J352 options.

sources.title: Jane Eyre by Charlotte Brontë
sources.rightsBasis: Underlying work is public domain in the UK; Project Gutenberg plain-text edition; only short attributed excerpts are retained.

evidence.locator: Chapter II; Jane is punished at Gateshead
evidence.anchor: Take her away to the red-room, and lock her in there.

evidence.locator: Chapter IX; Jane wakes beside Helen
evidence.anchor: I was asleep, and Helen was—dead.

evidence.locator: Chapter X; Jane seeks a post away from Lowood
evidence.anchor: I had a prospect of getting a new situation where the salary would be double what I now received

evidence.locator: Chapter XXVII; Jane resolves to leave
evidence.anchor: I must leave Adèle and Thornfield. I must part with you for my whole life

evidence.locator: Chapter XXXV; Jane hears Rochester’s call
evidence.anchor: I heard a voice somewhere cry— “Jane! Jane! Jane!”—nothing more.
Do you think I am an automaton?—a machine without feelings?
evidence.requiredAnswer: Do you think I am an automaton?—a machine without feelings?

evidence.locator: Chapter XII; Jane reflects on women’s confinement
evidence.anchor: Women are supposed to be very calm generally: but women feel just as men feel
evidence.requiredAnswer: Women are supposed to be very calm generally: but women feel just as men feel

evidence.anchor: The more solitary, the more friendless, the more unsustained I am, the more I will respect myself.
evidence.requiredAnswer: The more solitary, the more friendless, the more unsustained I am, the more I will respect myself.

evidence.locator: Chapter XVII; Jane disciplines her feelings
Chapter XXXVIII; Jane describes the marriage
evidence.anchor: I am my husband’s life as fully as he is mine.
evidence.requiredAnswer: I am my husband’s life as fully as he is mine.
